DN-essence

DNS rewrite rules for CoreDNS

Host Target Status Applied Actions
No rules yet. Add one above.

Add rule

How to use DN-essence

What is DN-essence?

DN-essence manages CoreDNS rewrite rules declaratively via Kubernetes custom resources (DNSRewrite). It solves the hairpin NAT problem: pods that need to reach a service by its public domain name instead of being routed outside the cluster and back in.

Every rule you create — whether from this UI or from kubectl — is stored as a DNSRewrite resource in Kubernetes. The controller watches those resources and keeps the CoreDNS ConfigMap in sync automatically.

UI ↔ CLI: single source of truth

Rules created from the UI are immediately visible via kubectl, and vice versa. There is no separate database — the Kubernetes API is the only source of truth.

Managing rules via kubectl

Create a rule

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dns-essence.io/v1
kind: DNSRewrite
metadata:
  name: my-app
spec:
  host: myapp.example.com
  target: myapp.default.svc.cluster.local
  enabled: true
EOF

List all rules

kubectl get dnsrewrites

Inspect a rule

kubectl get dnsrewrite my-app -o yaml

Disable a rule (without deleting it)

kubectl patch dnsrewrite my-app \
  --type merge -p '{"spec":{"enabled":false}}'

Delete a rule

kubectl delete dnsrewrite my-app

Field reference

FieldDescriptionExample
metadata.nameUnique identifier for the rule (Kubernetes name)my-app
spec.hostPublic FQDN to rewritemyapp.example.com
spec.targetIn-cluster Kubernetes service FQDNmyapp.default.svc.cluster.local
spec.enabledWhether the rule is active in CoreDNStrue / false

Status fields (read-only)

FieldMeaning
status.appliedtrue when the rule has been written to the CoreDNS ConfigMap
status.errorLast reconciliation error, if any

How the CoreDNS ConfigMap is managed

DN-essence only manages a clearly delimited section of the Corefile, marked with comments. Everything outside that section is never touched.

.:53 {
    # ... your existing CoreDNS config (untouched) ...

    # BEGIN dn-essence
    rewrite name myapp.example.com myapp.default.svc.cluster.local
    # END dn-essence
}
